compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator escalates to user instead of upgrading review tier when standard review exhausts cycles
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

During sprint execution of epic d000-2598 (batch 7), the standard-tier code reviewer (dso:code-reviewer-standard) failed to pass after 3+ revision cycles on a SKILL.md change. The orchestrator incorrectly escalated to the user with options (continue fixing / override / simplify) instead of following the review escalation protocol.

## Expected Behavior

Per CLAUDE.md feedback memory (feedback_review_escalation_tier.md) and the review workflow protocol: when the standard-tier review exhausts its resolution attempts, the orchestrator MUST escalate to the deep tier (3 parallel sonnet specialists + opus architectural synthesis), not present the user with options or ask for direction.

The escalation chain is: light → standard → deep (3 sonnet + opus arch). Only after the deep tier also fails should the orchestrator present remaining findings to the user.

## Actual Behavior

The orchestrator ran 4 cycles of standard-tier review (dso:code-reviewer-standard), then presented the user with 3 options instead of dispatching the deep tier reviewers. The user had to explicitly ask "Why didn't you escalate to opus as per the review protocol?" to trigger the correct behavior.

## Root Cause Analysis

The orchestrator conflated two distinct protocol steps:
1. **Revision cycle limit** (max 3 cycles of fix → re-review within a tier) — correctly tracked
2. **Tier escalation** (upgrade to a higher review tier when current tier consistently fails) — incorrectly skipped

The REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md revision protocol says "After exhausting cycles: present the user with..." but this applies AFTER all tiers are exhausted, not after a single tier fails. The feedback memory explicitly states: "Escalate to deep TIER (3 sonnet + opus arch), not just opus model override, when review resolution exhausts attempts."

## How to Apply

The sprint orchestrator (SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 7) and commit workflow (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 5) should both enforce: when standard-tier review fails after max_resolution_attempts, automatically escalate to deep tier before presenting to user. The user-facing escalation is the LAST resort, not the first.

## Severity

P2 — the workaround is the user asking for the correct behavior, but the failure mode wastes significant user attention and context on a decision the system should make automatically.

comments.body: Re-opened: initial fix applied without bot-psychologist investigation. These are LLM-behavioral bugs (skill/prompt behavioral gaps) that require proper bot-psychologist diagnosis per fix-bug SKILL.md dual-signal detection. The text changes committed are a first-pass fix — bot-psychologist should validate the behavioral diagnosis and confirm the fix addresses the actual root cause.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: ticket describes orchestrator behavioral gap (skipping tier escalation) + fix involves REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (workflow file in docs/workflows/).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
